question: Which phrase means 'to quickly become very emotional and start crying'?
My dad _____ come home at six in the morning and we'd have breakfast together. (repeated past action)
Which adjective means 'able to understand and share the feelings of others with care'?

Read: 'He was quite naughty and I used to mess about from time to time, sure. I mean, there was one time when I dressed up in my dad's clothes and went down the High Street pretending to be him!' This shows that the speaker:
choices: was always very quiet and well-behaved
was sometimes mischievous and playful as a child
never did anything silly or funny

question: My concentration span _____ very short when I was at school. (past state — ended)
Which phrase means 'to behave as an excellent model for others to follow'?
Bluetooth, _____ sounds like a dental problem, is actually a kind of wireless connection.
Which phrase means 'to go back to a state of harmony with someone after a disagreement'?
Read: 'They took it way too seriously and blew it completely out of proportion.' What did the speaker's parents do?
choices: they reacted calmly and ignored the prank
they laughed along with the speaker
they treated a small prank as if it were a very serious matter
they punished the speaker very fairly

question: Which relative pronoun is used for possession?
Which phrase describes 'failing an important school exam'?
choices: a clash between you and a sibling
a newborn baby in the family

question: My sister and I _____ always making up after every argument. (characteristic present behaviour)
What makes a paragraph 'coherent' according to the Active Writing box?
choices: it has as many sentences as possible
it has sentences in logical order supporting the topic sentence
it is very long and detailed

question: This is the man _____ won the golden prize.
Which phrase means 'to speak negatively about someone in their absence'?
She is really _____ about animals; they're so important to her.
Read: 'Around 74% of children under 10 use video-sharing sites online.' Is this sentence a fact or an opinion?
choices: a fact because it is a verifiable statistic
an opinion because it is about children
a fact because it mentions Jordan

question: When I was little, my brother _____ always show off in front of our relatives.
Which adjective describes someone who 'has a very positive, perhaps unrealistic view'?
Rewrite: 'The shop where I bought my computer has closed down.' (Defining relative clause, omitting the pronoun)
choices: The shop I bought my computer from has closed down.
The shop where I bought my computer from has closed down.
The shop which bought my computer has closed down.
The shop, where I bought my computer, has closed down.

question: He _____ go to the park every afternoon when he was a boy, but now he prefers staying indoors.
Which phrase means 'to behave as a great model that others follow'?
Read: 'I would argue that families generally remain as close as they ever were.' This sentence introduces:
choices: the writer's thesis or personal opinion

question: The woman _____ husband works abroad is my neighbour.
Which phrase means 'a heated argument or altercation'?
He's really _____; always telling everyone how great he is.
In 'You might think this is an overgeneralisation, but old people can't handle technology', this phrase signals:
choices: the writer acknowledges they may be exaggerating

question: Read: 'One night, a woman arrived at a workhouse. She gave birth to a baby but then sadly died.' What was the result for the baby?
choices: the baby was adopted by Mr Brownlow immediately
the baby was sent to a house for orphans
the baby was left at the workhouse with Fagin
the baby was sent to prison

question: Rewrite using a participle clause: 'The man who is waiting for the bus is my teacher.'
choices: The man waited for the bus is my teacher.
The man waiting for the bus is my teacher.
The man to wait for the bus is my teacher.
The man which waiting for the bus is my teacher.

question: Read: 'Oliver was shocked when he saw the boys take things from people's pockets.' What were the boys doing?
Which phrase means 'to have a serious disagreement that affects a relationship'?
She _____ constantly winding me up these days. I can't stand it!
Which phrase means 'to have someone whose success inspires you to do the same'?
Read: 'Mr Brownlow explained that Oliver's mother was a rich woman.' What does this new information suggest about Oliver's life?
choices: Oliver was always destined to be poor
Oliver had a right to an inheritance he had been kept from
Oliver's mother chose to live in a workhouse
Oliver knew about his mother's wealth all along

question: Which type of relative clause gives ESSENTIAL information and cannot be removed?
Which phrase means 'to be unable to tolerate someone any longer'?
He _____ constantly lose things when he was young. It frustrated everyone.
Which adjective describes someone who is 'unwilling to change their mind or accept different viewpoints'?
I had to go into the hall, _____ the phone was kept, and everyone could hear me.
Which phrase means 'to have deep contempt for someone and feel they are worthless'?
